questions.question: Define reflection of light.
questions.answer: Reflection of light is the process by which light rays falling on a surface bounce back into the same medium.

questions.question: State the laws of reflection of light.
questions.answer: 1. Angle of incidence is equal to angle of reflection. 2. Incident ray, reflected ray and normal lie in the same plane.

questions.question: What type of image is formed by a plane mirror?
questions.answer: A plane mirror forms a virtual, erect and laterally inverted image of the same size as the object.

questions.question: What is the difference between a concave and a convex mirror?
questions.answer: A concave mirror is curved inwards and converges light rays, while a convex mirror is curved outwards and diverges light rays.

questions.question: Define the radius of curvature of a spherical mirror.
questions.answer: Radius of curvature is the radius of the sphere of which the mirror forms a part.

questions.question: What is the relation between focal length and radius of curvature?
questions.answer: Focal length (f) is half the radius of curvature (R), i.e., f = R/2.

questions.question: Name one device that uses a concave mirror.
questions.answer: Concave mirrors are used in solar cookers to concentrate sunlight.

questions.question: Why are convex mirrors used as rear-view mirrors in vehicles?
questions.answer: Convex mirrors provide a wider field of view and form erect images of objects, making them suitable as rear-view mirrors.

questions.question: What is refraction of light?
questions.answer: Refraction is the bending of light when it passes obliquely from one transparent medium to another due to change in its speed.

questions.question: State Snell’s law.
questions.answer: The ratio of sine of angle of incidence to sine of angle of refraction is constant for a given pair of media.

questions.question: Define absolute refractive index.
questions.answer: Absolute refractive index of a medium is the ratio of speed of light in vacuum to speed of light in that medium.

questions.question: Why does a coin placed in water appear raised?
questions.answer: Due to refraction, light rays from the coin bend away from the normal when coming out of water, making the coin appear raised.

questions.question: What is lateral displacement?
questions.answer: Lateral displacement is the perpendicular distance between the path of the incident ray and the emergent ray when light passes through a glass slab.

questions.question: What is a convex lens?
questions.answer: A convex lens is thicker in the middle and thinner at the edges and converges parallel rays of light.

questions.question: What is a concave lens?
questions.answer: A concave lens is thinner in the middle and thicker at the edges and diverges parallel rays of light.

questions.question: What is the power of a lens?
questions.answer: Power of a lens is a measure of its ability to converge or diverge light rays. It is equal to the reciprocal of focal length in metres.

questions.question: State the mirror formula.
questions.answer: The mirror formula is 1/f = 1/v + 1/u, where f is focal length, v is image distance and u is object distance.

questions.question: What is the nature of image formed by a convex mirror?
questions.answer: A convex mirror always forms a virtual, erect and diminished image.

questions.question: Why do we see a rainbow after rain?
questions.answer: Rainbow is formed due to refraction, dispersion and total internal reflection of sunlight by water droplets.

questions.question: What is total internal reflection?
questions.answer: Total internal reflection occurs when light travelling in a denser medium is incident on a rarer medium at an angle greater than the critical angle and gets completely reflected back.

questions.question: Why do stars twinkle?
questions.answer: Twinkling of stars is caused by atmospheric refraction due to varying density layers in the atmosphere, changing the apparent position of stars.

questions.question: State the lens formula.
questions.answer: The lens formula is 1/f = 1/v – 1/u, where f is focal length, v is image distance and u is object distance.

questions.question: What is magnification?
questions.answer: Magnification is the ratio of the height of the image to the height of the object. It is also given by m = v/u.

questions.question: Why is a convex lens used as a magnifying glass?
questions.answer: When an object is placed between the optical centre and the focus of a convex lens, it forms a virtual, erect and magnified image.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of power of a lens?
questions.answer: The SI unit of power of a lens is dioptre (D).
